title: fraxinus longicuspis var. sambucina
intro: Fraxinus longicuspis var. sambucina, also known as the Manchurian ash, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, specifically China and Korea. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, sore throat, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Fraxinus longicuspis var. sambucina include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In traditional medicine, the bark and leaves of the plant are used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
